methods.description: Process of collecting pollen samples from various species for morphological study.

steps.description: Pollen from nine species, belonging to all six genera of Hemimerideae, was sampled in the herbarium of the Missouri Botanical Garden, St. Louis, Missouri, U.S.A. (MO).
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples from Missouri Botanical Garden herbarium

steps.description: Pollen from one species of Nemesia was sampled in the herbarium of the M. G. Kholodny Institute of Botany of the National Academy of Sciences of Ukraine, Kyiv (Kiev), Ukraine (KW).
steps.searchable_summary: collect Nemesia pollen samples from Ukrainian herbarium

methods.description: Study of pollen morphology using light microscopy and scanning electron microscopy to understand structural details.

steps.description: Pollen morphology was studied using light microscopy (LM) and scanning electron microscopy (SEM), following the techniques described in previous articles.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen samples for LM and SEM analysis

steps.description: For LM (Biolar, × 700), the pollen was acetolysed and mounted on slides following Erdtman (1952).
steps.searchable_summary: acetolyse and mount pollen for light microscopy

steps.description: For size determinations, 20 measurements were taken along the polar (P) and equatorial (E) axes for each species.
steps.searchable_summary: measure pollen size along polar and equatorial axes

steps.description: For SEM (JSM-6060LA), pollen grains were treated with 96 % ethanol, then sputter-coated with gold.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen for SEM by ethanol treatment and gold sputter-coating

steps.description: The obtained micrographs were minimally edited with Adobe Photoshop 6.0 to enhance the images, with no alteration of the images themselves.
steps.searchable_summary: edit SEM micrographs for clarity using Photoshop
